feu: noun. Land held in feudal tenure.
few: determiner. An indefinite, but usually small, number of.
floe: noun. A low, flat mass of floating ice.
flop: verb. To fall heavily due to lack of energy.
flour: noun. Powder obtained by grinding or milling cereal grains, especially wheat, or other foodstuffs such as soybeans and potatoes, and used
flow: noun. Movement in people or things characterized with a continuous motion, involving either a non solid mass or a multitude.
flower: noun. A colorful, conspicuous structure associated with angiosperms, frequently scented and attracting various insects, and which may or
flue: noun. A pipe or duct that carries gaseous combustion products away from the point of combustion (such as a furnace).
fop: noun. A vain man; a dandy.
for: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fore.
fore: adjective. Former; occurring earlier (in some order); previous.
foul: adjective. Covered with, or containing unclean matter; dirty.
four: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 4.
fro: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Old French.
froe: noun. A cleaving tool for splitting cask staves and shingles from a block of wood.
frow: noun. A woman; a wife, especially a Dutch or German one.
fuel: noun. Substance consumed to provide energy through combustion, or through chemical or nuclear reaction.
fur: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Friulian.
furl: verb. To lower, roll up and secure (something, such as a sail or flag)
leu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (New Guinea).
lop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lopa.
lope: verb. To travel an easy pace with long strides.
loper: noun. One who or that which lopes; a runner; a leaper.
lore: noun. All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.
loup: noun. A mass or ball of iron in a pasty condition, beaten by the trip hammer or rolls.
loupe: noun. A magnifying glass, usually mounted in an eyepiece, often used by jewellers and watchmakers.
lour: verb. To frown; to look sullen.
low: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tampias Lobu.
lower: adjective. comparative form of low: more low
ole: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Olekha.
ope: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Old Persian.

plower: noun. One who plows; one who works land with a plow.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
pouf: noun. A headdress for women popular in 18th century France.
pour: verb. To cause (liquid, or liquid-like substance) to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into it.
pow: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for San Felipe Otlaltepec Popoloca.
power: noun. The ability to do or undergo something.
powerful: adjective. Having, or capable of exerting, power or influence.
prole: noun. A member of the proletariat; a proletarian.
prow: noun. The front part of a vessel.
prowl: verb. To rove over, through, or about in a stealthy manner; especially, to search in, as for prey or booty.
pul: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of an Afghan afghani.
puler: noun. Someone who pules; a whinger or complainer

pure: adjective. Free of flaws or imperfections; unsullied.

purl: noun. A particular stitch in knitting in which the working yarn is pulled through an existing stitch from front to back.
reflow: verb. To flow back again; to flow once more.
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
row: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dela-Oenale.
rowel: noun. A small spiked wheel on the end of a spur of certain types.
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
woe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Woleaian.

wolf: noun. Canis lupus; the largest wild member of the canine subfamily.
wolfer: noun. One who devours food greedily; one who wolfs down food.
wop: noun. An person of Italian descent.
